docs.title: Welcome
docs.text: Welcome to AP Chemistry, an interactive intelligent textbook designed for high school students seeking college credit.

docs.title: About the AP Chemistry Textbook
docs.text:
This course covers all nine units of the College Board's AP Chemistry Course and Exam Description, organized into 18 chapters spanning 500 concepts. Unlike traditional textbooks, every chapter includes interactive MicroSims, hover-enabled diagrams, and a learning graph that maps concept dependencies so you always know what to study next.

The textbook emphasizes:

  • Conceptual understanding over memorization
  • Mathematical modeling of chemical systems
  • Interactive simulations for hands-on exploration
  • Visual learning with molecular structures, reaction diagrams, and flowcharts

docs.title: Who This Textbook Is For
docs.text:
• High school students preparing for the AP Chemistry Exam
  • Students planning to major in chemistry, engineering, medicine, or other STEM fields
  • Motivated learners seeking a rigorous, college-level chemistry experience
  • Anyone with a first-year chemistry course and Algebra II background

docs.title: How to Use This Textbook
docs.text:
Use the navigation menu on the left to explore:

  • Chapters — 18 chapters of detailed content with embedded MicroSims
  • Search - Note the search form in the upper right corner of the web pages
  • MicroSims — Interactive simulations for hands-on concept exploration
  • Learning Graph — Visual map of 500 concepts and their dependencies
  • Glossary — Key terms and definitions
  • FAQ - Frequently Asked Questions
  • References - References are also given at the end of each chapter
  • Appendices — Reference tables and supplemental material

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Start with Chapter 1: Foundations of Chemistry to begin your journey. Let's react!

docs.title: How Was This Book Generated?
docs.text: This book was generated by using a series of Agent Skills created by Dan McCreary. Dan used mostly Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 with some help from OpenAI ChatGPT 5.2. Dan also reused MicroSims from other intelligent textbooks.
